title: Acidoton baillonianus
taxonomy: Acidoton baillonianus is a species within the family Rhizophoraceae. This family consists of mangrove trees and shrubs. Acidoton baillonianus is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It belongs to the order Malpighiales, which includes a diverse group of flowering plants. Acidoton baillonianus is often classified as a type of mangrove tree due to its adaptability to coastal and riverine environments.

intro: Acidoton baillonianus, also known as Guarea glabra, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, belonging to the family Meliaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.
